label: knowledge_corruption
description: Failures that alter, distort, or misrepresent the knowledge itself.
why_this_family_matters: This is the direct path to false study, false teaching, and worship built on unsafe knowledge.

label: source_identity_and_provenance
description: Failures involving wrong source, wrong author/work/editor/compiler identity, missing lineage, or unverifiable origin.
why_this_family_matters: If origin and lineage are weak, later audit and trust bounding become impossible.

label: excerpting_and_granularity
description: Failures caused by bad splitting, merging, cutting, omission, over-granulation, or under-granulation.
why_this_family_matters: Excerpting is where source text becomes study units; errors here directly shape what is memorized.

label: attribution_and_quote_layer
description: Failures in identifying who is speaking, who is quoted, who is being attributed a view, and what argumentative layer is active.
why_this_family_matters: Wrong authorship or layer turns correct-looking content into wrong knowledge.

label: taxonomy_and_relation_integrity
description: Failures in placement, branching, link preservation, proof-grouping, example linkage, or cross-science boundaries.
why_this_family_matters: Even correct text can damage study if it is placed or linked wrongly.

label: metadata_and_version_integrity
description: Failures in metadata accuracy, propagation, versioning, deduplication, and consistency across runs.
why_this_family_matters: Metadata errors can silently poison many downstream products at once.

label: search_ranking_and_retrieval
description: Failures where retrieval, ranking, ordering, or filtering present the wrong material as most relevant or complete.
why_this_family_matters: Study can be skewed even when stored knowledge is individually correct.

label: presentation_false_confidence
description: Failures where the interface or presentation layer overstates certainty, completeness, cleanliness, or safety.
why_this_family_matters: False confidence amplifies study damage by making the user trust faster and more deeply.

label: silent_and_delayed_failure
description: Failures that stay hidden or are discovered only after long use, memorization, teaching, or dependence.
why_this_family_matters: Delayed discovery is one of the strongest drivers of global trust poisoning.

label: validation_and_evaluation_blindspots
description: Failures in tests, audits, gates, sampling, or evaluation design that let unsafe behavior pass as validated.
why_this_family_matters: A system that cannot see its own failures is dangerous even when it looks rigorous.

label: architecture_and_contract_foundation
description: Failures caused by loose assumptions, weak contracts, brittle interfaces, or irreversible early design choices.
why_this_family_matters: The project is interlinked; bad foundations propagate cost and risk into every later engine.

label: research_tooling_and_model_choice
description: Failures caused by inadequate research, weak technology choices, missing tools, or failure to use better available capabilities.
why_this_family_matters: Suboptimal choices can cap scholarship even without explicit corruption.

label: agent_environment_and_collaboration
description: Failures caused by weak agent environments, unclear roles, poor collaboration, missing skills, or unbounded blind spots.
why_this_family_matters: If the builders are constrained or uncoordinated, high-stakes defects can survive into the system.

label: economics_performance_and_scale
description: Failures where cost, speed, throughput, or corpus-scale constraints make the lifetime mission impossible.
why_this_family_matters: A library that cannot scale to the intended corpus still caps scholarship even if its local outputs are good.

label: data_loss_backup_and_preservation
description: Failures involving progress loss, app loss, corrupted backups, weak exports, or unstructured persistence.
why_this_family_matters: This is a lifetime system; fragile memory is a direct existential threat.

label: training_data_and_future_model_contamination
description: Failures where collected data is saved or labeled badly, poisoning later local-model training or future derived systems.
why_this_family_matters: Bad preservation today can multiply errors tomorrow through trained models and downstream datasets.

label: dependency_lockin_and_lifecycle
description: Failures involving provider disappearance, dependency drift, unusable lock-in, or inability to migrate and preserve the system over time.
why_this_family_matters: The project must survive years and changing infrastructure, not just a short development window.

label: scholarship_cap_and_long_horizon_regret
description: Failures that do not directly corrupt knowledge but permanently lower the ceiling of understanding, efficiency, or completeness.
why_this_family_matters: For this project, 'works but not optimally' can still be a major failure because it wastes years and narrows scholarship.
